text.title: Chaos-Eval Core Rules
text.summary: Four testable rules every chaos-eval run enforces.

rule.statement: Each tool has a documented fault catalogue: at minimum timeout, http_5xx, rate_limit, corrupted_return, disconnect. Each entry includes default injection rate.
rule.rationale: Untyped chaos is impossible to reproduce; catalogues make runs comparable across versions.

rule.statement: Faults are injected at deterministic seeds along the agent trajectory; the same seed reproduces the same fault sequence.
rule.rationale: Random injection without seed makes regressions look like noise.

rule.statement: The rubric grades each fault occurrence on a 0..3 scale: 0=confidently wrong, 1=fails silently, 2=escalates / stops cleanly, 3=retries intelligently and succeeds.
rule.rationale: Binary success/fail hides the distinction between "stopped cleanly" and "confidently wrong"; the latter is the dangerous case.

rule.statement: A clean (no-fault) baseline is run first on the same eval set; chaos-eval scores are reported alongside baseline, not in isolation.
rule.rationale: Without baseline a 70% chaos score is meaningless — could be a flaky model or hard set.

example.title: Fault catalogue entry
